page: Vultr vbm-64c-2048gb-8-l40-gpu is a dedicated bare metal server built on the x86_64 architecture. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon 6448H processor containing 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs, operating at a base clock speed of 2.4 GHz. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures that compute resources are fully isolated and free from virtualization overhead or multi-tenant interference.

The server is configured with 2048 GB of system memory, translating to 16 GB of RAM per physical core. For parallel processing and acceleration, the platform integrates eight NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture. These GPUs provide a combined total of 384 GB of VRAM, enabling the execution of large-scale datasets and complex neural network architectures directly in graphics memory.

Storage is supported by a 960 GB local SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare metal deployment model eliminates hypervisor tax, maximizing the return on hardware investment by delivering direct physical access to the underlying silicon. This resource density is particularly advantageous for workloads that require continuous, heavy utilization of both CPU and GPU resources.

This hardware configuration is tailored for workloads such as large language model inference, artificial intelligence training, complex 3D rendering, data science pipelines, and scientific computing simulations.
description: Vultr vbm-64c-2048gb-8-l40-gpu is an x86_64 bare metal server designed for dense GPU-accelerated workloads. It features an Intel Xeon 6448H processor with 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs operating at 2.4 GHz, paired with 2048 GB of system memory. The server is equipped with eight NVIDIA L40S GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 384 GB of VRAM. Storage is provided by a 960 GB local SSD, and the configuration includes one public IPv4 address. This dedicated bare metal environment offers high resource density and direct hardware access, making it cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive parallel processing power without virtualization overhead. It is engineered for demanding tasks such as large-scale artificial intelligence training, large language model inference, complex 3D rendering pipelines, and scientific computing.
og_description: Vultr vbm-64c-2048gb-8-l40-gpu is a bare metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 2048 GB RAM, and eight NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 384 GB VRAM, optimized for AI training and scientific computing.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-64c-2048gb-8-l40-gpu bare metal server features 128 vCPUs, 2 TB RAM, and 8 NVIDIA L40S GPUs for AI and scientific computing.
tagline: A dense bare metal server equipped with eight NVIDIA L40S GPUs and two terabytes of memory for parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs with 384 GB total VRAM
Intel Xeon 6448H processor with 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs
2048 GB of system memory providing 16 GB per core
960 GB of local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address

Best-fit for AI/ML training, LLM inference, and scientific computing
